metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_766
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This paper describes how "knockdown of cortactin by short-interfering RNA (siRNA) results in a decrease of dendritic spines in hippocampal neurons, whereas overexpression of cortactin causes elongation of spines. In response to synaptic stimulation and NMDA receptor activation, cortactin redistributes rapidly from spines to dendritic shaft, correlating with remodeling of the actin cytoskeleton, implicating cortactin in the activity-dependent regulation of spine morphogenesis."

Thus, cortactin seems implicated in spine morphology maintenance and activity-induced reorganization. Therefore, I chose the GO term "regulation of synapse organization" to combine these two functions.. Experimental description: from M&M:
"Hippocampal neuron cultures were prepared from embryonic day 18-19 rat embryos as described previously (Brewer et al., 1993)."

And, "For all constructs except W22A and W22A tandem, murine wild-type cortactin was used as template (gift of J. Thomas Parson, University of Virginia, Charlottesville, VA)."

12/6/2017
Although species of overexpressed protein is not stated ('murine') I allow annotation to rat based on the RNAi experiments in rat neurons.
